text: Fix watchdog staying connected to wrong project on switch/reload
description: When switching projects or reloading with a different project, the wrapped-agent WebSocket could remain connected to the previous project's session. Root cause: handleProjectSwitch only ran inside WatchdogPanel (v-if gated), so hidden panels never disconnected. Fix: move watcher to App.vue, proper socket teardown, buffer clear, session path guard.

reflections.context: User reported wrapped-agent session could stay connected to the wrong project after reload or project switch.
reflections.outcome: Root cause was handleProjectSwitch only in WatchdogPanel (v-if). Moved to App.vue, hardened socket teardown, cleared buffers, added cross-project connect guard. 3 new store tests + existing WatchdogPanel tests pass.
reflections.effectiveness: High — small targeted fix for a subtle lifecycle bug; tests lock in teardown/reconnect behavior.
reflections.lesson: Pinia store lifecycle hooks must not live only inside conditionally-mounted components — global side effects (WebSocket) need app-level watchers.
reflections.pattern: Any v-if gated UI that owns reconnect logic should delegate switch handling to a always-mounted parent.

status.comment: Follow-up fix: agent dropdown now uses switchAgentType (not full disconnect); project switch force-reconnects when carrying active socket despite autoConnectToExistingSession=false. 10 watchdog tests pass.
